name: Segun Banji
bio:
Segun Banji is the founder and Managing Director of Mcmoren Multiservices Limited, a company he established with a vision to bridge the gap between world-class service delivery and the Nigerian business environment. With over two decades of operational experience spanning logistics, international trade facilitation, and business management, Segun has built Mcmoren into a trusted name across multiple industry sectors.

His leadership philosophy is grounded in the TRICE values that define the organisation — Trust, Reliability, Integrity, Customer Satisfaction, and Excellence. Under his direction, Mcmoren has grown from a small freight operation into a full-service multiservices company with active engagement across transport, customs brokerage, freight forwarding, fashion, and carpentry through its McAdur Imaginations subsidiary.

Segun is deeply committed to developing Nigerian enterprises, empowering local talent, and delivering service standards that compete at an international level. He holds professional certifications in logistics management and international trade, and continues to drive the company's expansion into new sectors and markets.
responsibilities: Overall strategic direction and long-term planning for Mcmoren Multiservices Limited
Leadership of executive and senior management teams across all business units
Client and partner relationship management at the highest level
Oversight of all logistics, customs, and freight forwarding operations
Governance of the McAdur Imaginations fashion and carpentry division
Business development, new market entry, and key account acquisition
Ensuring organisational alignment with the company's TRICE values
Financial oversight and accountability to stakeholders

name: Amaka Okafor
bio: Amaka Okafor oversees all operational activities across Mcmoren's logistics and customs divisions. She brings over 12 years of field experience in Nigerian port operations and freight management, ensuring every shipment is executed efficiently and in full compliance with Nigerian Customs Service regulations.
responsibilities: Day-to-day operations management across all logistics corridors
Team supervision for field operations and port clearance staff
SLA management and client delivery performance reporting
Process optimisation and operational standards enforcement
Liaison with NCS, NPA, and relevant regulatory authorities

name: Chukwuemeka Eze
bio: Chukwuemeka Eze is a certified customs broker and trade compliance expert with extensive knowledge of Nigerian import and export regulations. He manages all documentation, duty assessment, and agency liaisons to ensure Mcmoren's clients face zero compliance exposure.
responsibilities: Management of all Form M, PAAR, and customs declaration filings
Coordination with SON, NAFDAC, NESREA for regulated imports
Duty optimisation and tariff classification advisory
Staff training on customs compliance and procedural updates
Risk assessment for new trade corridors and commodity types

name: Fatima Ibrahim
bio: Fatima Ibrahim leads the McAdur Imaginations division — the creative heart of Mcmoren Multiservices. A trained fashion designer and textile expert, she oversees both production and training programmes in bespoke fashion and custom carpentry, developing skilled creatives across Nigeria.
responsibilities: Creative direction and product quality for all McAdur bespoke fashion outputs
Management of fashion design and carpentry training programmes
Client consultation for custom garment and furniture commissions
Sourcing of materials and artisan partnerships
Building the McAdur brand in the creative economy

name: Daniel Okonkwo
bio: Daniel Okonkwo manages Mcmoren's nationwide fleet operations and inter-city freight scheduling. His expertise in route optimisation and fleet management ensures cargo moves efficiently from port to final destination, even across Nigeria's most challenging delivery corridors.
responsibilities: Fleet scheduling, dispatch, and real-time tracking coordination
Driver management, welfare, and compliance with transport regulations
Route planning for high-volume and time-critical freight movements
Vehicle maintenance oversight and fleet renewal planning
Partnership management with third-party transport providers

name: Ngozi Adeyemi
bio: Ngozi Adeyemi is the first point of contact for all Mcmoren clients, ensuring every engagement starts with clarity and confidence. She manages client onboarding, enquiry handling, and ongoing account management, translating complex logistics and trade requirements into clear, actionable plans.
responsibilities: Client enquiry management and first-response coordination
Onboarding new clients and briefing operations teams
Account management and regular service performance updates
Resolution of client concerns and escalations
Collecting and acting on client feedback for service improvement
